req.title: Consent management (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers consent grant/revoke lifecycle with a 15-minute SLA for cache invalidation. I selected: (1) EP-positive for a valid grant flow confirming 201 storage; (2) NEG-negative for data access after revoke within the 15-min SLA window, which must be denied; (3) BVA-boundary for timestamp at exactly t=0s after revoke (instantaneous enforcement boundary); (4) STC for the full Active→Revoked→Active state cycle; (5) DT for the matrix of consent status × accessor role producing the correct HTTP response code. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and placeholders, ≤40 LOC in the executable field.

tests.name: test_grant_consent_returns_201_and_stores_resource
preconditions: No prior Consent resource exists for this patient/category pair
actions: POST /Consent with a valid FHIR Consent payload (status=active, provision.type=permit)
GET /Consent/{id} to confirm the resource is persisted
expected: Subsequent GET returns HTTP 200 with status=active

tests.name: test_data_access_denied_within_sla_after_revoke
preconditions: An active Consent resource already exists for the patient
A downstream data-sharing resource is accessible under active consent
actions: Immediately (within 15 minutes) attempt GET /Patient/{id}/$everything as a third-party accessor
expected: PATCH returns HTTP 200 or 204 confirming revoke
Immediate subsequent data access returns HTTP 403 Forbidden
Response body indicates consent-denied or equivalent OperationOutcome
trace: R-HLT-040: revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes
R-HLT-040: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

tests.name: test_consent_revoke_enforced_at_boundary_t0_seconds
preconditions: System clock is synchronised; test executor can measure elapsed time
actions: Record timestamp T_revoke immediately before PATCH /Consent/{id} with status=inactive
Immediately after PATCH succeeds, send GET /Patient/{id}/$everything (elapsed ≈ 0 s)
Assert access is denied at the exact lower boundary of enforcement window
expected: GET at T_revoke + ~0s returns HTTP 403 (boundary: enforcement starts immediately)
Elapsed time between PATCH and GET is less than 5 seconds confirming t=0 boundary
trace: R-HLT-040: revoked consent enforced within 15 minutes (BVA lower bound = 0 s)
R-HLT-040: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested

tests.name: test_consent_state_transition_active_revoked_active
preconditions: System supports re-granting consent after revocation
actions: Assert each transition returns correct HTTP code and final state is active
trace: R-HLT-040: patient shall grant or revoke consent
R-HLT-040: consent stored at each state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<base_url>"

@pytest.fixture
def patient_headers():
    return <get_patient_auth_headers>()

def _consent_body():
    return {
        "resourceType": "Consent",
        "status": "active",
        "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "LOINC-59284-0"}]}],
        "patient": {"reference": "Patient/<patient_id>"},
        "provision": {"type": "permit"}
    }

def test_consent_state_transition_active_revoked_active(patient_headers):
    r1 = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent", json=_consent_body(), headers=patient_headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    cid = r1.json()["id"]

    r2 = requests.patch(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{cid}", json={"status": "inactive"}, headers=patient_headers)
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 204)
    r2g = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{cid}", headers=patient_headers)
    assert r2g.json()["status"] == "inactive"

    r3 = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent", json=_consent_body(), headers=patient_headers)
    assert r3.status_code == 201
    assert r3.json()["status"] == "active"
    <cleanup_consent>(cid, patient_headers)
    <cleanup_consent>(r3.json()["id"], patient_headers)

tests.name: test_consent_decision_table_status_vs_role
actions: Case A: active consent + third-party accessor → GET data → expect 200
Case B: inactive consent + third-party accessor → GET data → expect 403
Case C: inactive consent + admin accessor → GET data → expect 200 (admin bypass)
Assert each combination returns the mapped HTTP status
trace: R-HLT-040: downstream caches invalidated per role
